turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="bg" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#0b3d91"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#05091a"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="bodyGrad" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#ffffff"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#cbd3db"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="glass" cx="50%" cy="40%" r="60%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#aee5ff"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#4aa6d6"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <radialGradient id="flameGrad" cx="50%" cy="30%" r="70%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff176"/>
      <stop offset="50%" stop-color="#ff8a00"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#c21807"/>
    </radialGradient>
  </defs>

  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="url(#bg)"/>

  <!-- stars -->
  <g fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.9">
    <circle cx="60" cy="60" r="1.8"/>
    <circle cx="120" cy="40" r="1.4"/>
    <circle cx="420" cy="80" r="1.6"/>
    <circle cx="350" cy="40" r="1.2"/>
    <circle cx="460" cy="150" r="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="80" cy="140" r="1.3"/>
  </g>

  <!-- rocket -->
  <g>
    <!-- fins -->
    <path d="M216 320 L160 360 L216 340 Z" fill="#d32f2f"/>
    <path d="M296 320 L352 360 L296 340 Z" fill="#d32f2f"/>

    <!-- body -->
    <rect x="216" y="120" width="80" height="200" rx="40" ry="40" fill="url(#bodyGrad)" stroke="#777" stroke-width="2"/>

    <!-- nose cone -->
    <path d="M256 64 L296 120 L216 120 Z" fill="#ec407a" stroke="#aa2a4a" stroke-width="2"/>

    <!-- window -->
    <circle cx="256" cy="200" r="28" fill="url(#glass)" stroke="#2d6f9e" stroke-width="4"/>

    <!-- engine bell -->
    <ellipse cx="256" cy="356" rx="28" ry="14" fill="#424242"/>

    <!-- flame -->
    <path d="M256 370 C246 390 232 418 256 456 C280 418 266 390 256 370 Z" fill="url(#flameGrad)" opacity="0.95"/>

    <!-- small highlight on body -->
    <path d="M232 140 C236 130 246 125 256 125 C266 125 276 129 280 139 L280 140 C270 132 242 132 232 140 Z" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.15"/>
  </g>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{"svg":"<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="bg" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#0b3d91"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#05091a"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="bodyGrad" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#ffffff"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#cbd3db"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="glass" cx="50%" cy="40%" r="60%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#aee5ff"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#4aa6d6"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <radialGradient id="flameGrad" cx="50%" cy="30%" r="70%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff176"/>
      <stop offset="50%" stop-color="#ff8a00"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#c21807"/>
    </radialGradient>
  </defs>

  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="url(#bg)"/>

  <!-- stars -->
  <g fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.9">
    <circle cx="60" cy="60" r="1.8"/>
    <circle cx="120" cy="40" r="1.4"/>
    <circle cx="420" cy="80" r="1.6"/>
    <circle cx="350" cy="40" r="1.2"/>
    <circle cx="460" cy="150" r="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="80" cy="140" r="1.3"/>
  </g>

  <!-- rocket -->
  <g>
    <!-- fins -->
    <path d="M216 320 L160 360 L216 340 Z" fill="#d32f2f"/>
    <path d="M296 320 L352 360 L296 340 Z" fill="#d32f2f"/>

    <!-- body -->
    <rect x="216" y="120" width="80" height="200" rx="40" ry="40" fill="url(#bodyGrad)" stroke="#777" stroke-width="2"/>

    <!-- nose cone -->
    <path d="M256 64 L296 120 L216 120 Z" fill="#ec407a" stroke="#aa2a4a" stroke-width="2"/>

    <!-- window -->
    <circle cx="256" cy="200" r="28" fill="url(#glass)" stroke="#2d6f9e" stroke-width="4"/>

    <!-- engine bell -->
    <ellipse cx="256" cy="356" rx="28" ry="14" fill="#424242"/>

    <!-- flame -->
    <path d="M256 370 C246 390 232 418 256 456 C280 418 266 390 256 370 Z" fill="url(#flameGrad)" opacity="0.95"/>

    <!-- small highlight on body -->
    <path d="M232 140 C236 130 246 125 256 125 C266 125 276 129 280 139 L280 140 C270 132 242 132 232 140 Z" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.15"/>
  </g>
</svg>"}
message.reasoning:
**Formatting JSON for SVG**

I'm working on creating JSON that includes an SVG string. I need to ensure that any quotes and control characters inside the string are correctly escaped. It's important to remember that I can't use triple quotes, so I’ll craft a single-line or multi-line string with backslashes to escape quotes properly.

I'll assemble the SVG content while checking for validity, including attributes like the xmlns. Let's get that final JSON ready!**Creating SVG Components**

I’m composing an SVG file with various elements, starting with the XML declaration and defining gradients for the background, body, glass, and flame. Then, I'm including a rectangle as the background and adding stars represented by small circles with different sizes and positions. 

For the rocket, I’m defining the fins, body, and nose cone, including window details and engine bell. It’s important to ensure all elements are correctly placed and have the right gradient fills. Let's finalize this SVG!**Finalizing SVG JSON**

I’ve completed the SVG with a small highlight on the body, and now it’s time to convert this into proper JSON. I need to ensure that double quotes in the JSON string are escaped, along with newlines represented as \n.

I should be cautious about using backslashes to escape the characters correctly. It’s important to produce a well-formatted JSON string with minimal escaping, ensuring that I use double quotes around the entire string while properly escaping any internal double quotes. Let’s get this JSON finalized!

reasoning_details.summary:
**Formatting JSON for SVG**

I'm working on creating JSON that includes an SVG string. I need to ensure that any quotes and control characters inside the string are correctly escaped. It's important to remember that I can't use triple quotes, so I’ll craft a single-line or multi-line string with backslashes to escape quotes properly.

I'll assemble the SVG content while checking for validity, including attributes like the xmlns. Let's get that final JSON ready!**Creating SVG Components**

I’m composing an SVG file with various elements, starting with the XML declaration and defining gradients for the background, body, glass, and flame. Then, I'm including a rectangle as the background and adding stars represented by small circles with different sizes and positions. 

For the rocket, I’m defining the fins, body, and nose cone, including window details and engine bell. It’s important to ensure all elements are correctly placed and have the right gradient fills. Let's finalize this SVG!**Finalizing SVG JSON**

I’ve completed the SVG with a small highlight on the body, and now it’s time to convert this into proper JSON. I need to ensure that double quotes in the JSON string are escaped, along with newlines represented as \n.

I should be cautious about using backslashes to escape the characters correctly. It’s important to produce a well-formatted JSON string with minimal escaping, ensuring that I use double quotes around the entire string while properly escaping any internal double quotes. Let’s get this JSON finalized!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}

response.rawBody: {"id":"gen-1772255568-cBA9vMuOZ7AVDBgjUaRZ","provider":"Azure","model":"openai/gpt-5-mini","object":"chat.completion","created":1772255568,"choices":[{"logprobs":null,"finish_reason":"stop","native_finish_reason":"completed","index":0,"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"{\"guesses\":[\"rocket\",\"spaceship\",\"rocketship\",\"shuttle\",\"spacecraft\",\"booster\",\"capsule\",\"launch\",\"orbiter\",\"satellite\",\"probe\",\"missile\",\"vessel\",\"ship\",\"module\",\"engine\",\"thruster\",\"pod\",\"comet\",\"astronaut\"]}","refusal":null,"reasoning":"**Generating guess list**\n\nI need to output a JSON with a list of 20 one-word guesses based on the drawing of a rocket ship. The top guess should definitely be \"rocket,\" followed by \"spaceship,\" \"rocketship,\" \"shuttle,\" \"spacecraft,\" and others. I need to avoid repetition and make sure they're all simple, one word, and without spaces or punctuation. Some possible words include \"booster,\" \"capsule,\" \"missile,\" \"vessel,\" \"probe,\" and \"satellite.\" But then I realize \"missile\" might not fit since it implies weaponry. Let's finalize that list!**Finalizing guess list**\n\nI need exactly 20 unique one-word guesses ordered by confidence. message.reasoning:
**Generating guess list**

I need to output a JSON with a list of 20 one-word guesses based on the drawing of a rocket ship. The top guess should definitely be "rocket," followed by "spaceship," "rocketship," "shuttle," "spacecraft," and others. I need to avoid repetition and make sure they're all simple, one word, and without spaces or punctuation. Some possible words include "booster," "capsule," "missile," "vessel," "probe," and "satellite." But then I realize "missile" might not fit since it implies weaponry. Let's finalize that list!**Finalizing guess list**

I need exactly 20 unique one-word guesses ordered by confidence. The top guess is "rocket," followed by "spaceship," "rocketship" (which is a bit redundant, so I’ll skip that), and then "shuttle," "spacecraft," "booster," and "capsule." 

I’ll add "launch," "orbiter," "satellite," "probe," "missile," "vessel," "ship," "module," and "engine." However, "comet" and "rocketman" aren’t ideal choices since "rocketman" refers to a person. I’ll make sure to keep it focused on valid, one-word guesses!**Compiling guess list**

I need to order the final list of 20 unique items by decreasing confidence. The list includes "rocket," "spaceship," "rocketship," "shuttle," "spacecraft," "booster," "capsule," "launch," "orbiter," "satellite," "probe," "missile," "vessel," "ship," "module," "engine," "thruster," "pod," "comet," and "astronaut." 

All entries are one word and in lowercase without punctuation. I’ll format this as JSON, ensuring it meets the requirements of being one word, no spaces. Now, I’m all set to return the JSON!
